name: Agent: Copywriting
summary: Turns a guided interview into copy that's ready to publish — for any brand, any market, never translated word for word.
objectives: Turn a guided interview into channel-ready copy — for any brand, any market — instead of a blank page.
Stop a copy claim that can't be backed before it ships — a check that fails closed, not one that hopes someone notices.
Re-create copy for each market so it reads native, never translate it word for word.

outcomes.text: Anyone can download the agent and run it: a guided interview becomes copy for organic social, ads, email, landing pages and video scripts.
Copy with a claim that can't be backed does not ship. The claims check fails closed — an unverified stat or promise holds the copy until a person validates it.
It never translates word for word. Copy is rebuilt for each market so it reads native — Latin America, Spain, Brazil, Turkey — with a person approving the adaptation.
It runs for any brand, not one. The published agent carries no client's private setup — the brand's own configuration lives apart, so the same agent serves whoever loads it.
It proved itself end to end before going live: a full dry run across every channel passed, and campaign copy now routes to it.

milestones.title: Put the whole copy craft into one portable agent — principles, workflow, the interview
Build the claims gate that fails closed, so unbacked copy can't ship
Write the market modules so copy is rebuilt, never translated, for each region
Set up the first brand as its own configuration, kept apart from the shared agent
Prove it end to end with a full dry run across every channel
Publish the agent so anyone can download and run it, with no private setup inside
Route campaign copy tasks to it in the shared workspace
